body_txt:
Hometown isn't doomed ? Doom it yourself! Whether it's an accident, revenge or just plain anger , characters sometimes display disturbing tendencies toward destroying their own hometowns, countries, planets or even universes. Bonus point if they simultaneously kill their parents . This is more frequent with villains, especially the more vile and over-the-top ones. Sometimes it's to demonstrate how disturbed they are, other times it's a Start of Darkness event for them. Or maybe they just really liked the sound of being the Last of His Kind . It doesn't count if only the house is destroyed, in attempts to kill the hero or just to spite them . It also doesn't count if a broader war is involved, unless the character's actions directly created that war. If the character is generally portrayed sympathetically when doing this, it overlaps with Destroy the Abusive Home . See also The End of the World as We Know It , Genocide from the Inside , Mike Nelson, Destroyer of Worlds , Bloodbath Villain Origin , With Great Power Comes Great Responsibility . Compare Let the Past Burn .
Contrast Humanizing Moment .
